Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Typically the process includes posting a job ad screening candidates conducting interviews making a hiring decision onboarding adding to payroll and closing the case There might be typical order Post Screen Interview Decide Onboard Payroll Close

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Model1 edges are Post Screen Screen Decide Screen Interview Decide Onboard Onboard Payroll Payroll Close so after Screen both Decide and Interview are allowed and there is no ordering between Interview and Decide

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This means the model allows deciding before conducting interviews and even onboarding before interviews because Onboard depends on Decide and Interview has no constraint to occur before Decide or Onboard

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: That is an anomaly because the typical process requires interviews before the hiring decision

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Interview in Model1 has only Screen as predecessor and no successor so it could occur very late even after Close in a linearization of the partial order which is illogical for a hire process

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Model1 still keeps all core activities mandatory and preserves the chain Decide Onboard Payroll Close which matches the standard tail of the process

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Model2 has Post Screen Post Interview Interview Decide Decide loop onboarding loop onboarding xor payroll xor payroll Close and no edge from Screen to Interview or Decide

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So screening is disconnected from the actual selection decision and interviews can start right after posting without screening

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: That is anomalous because screening normally precedes interviews and informs the later hiring decision

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Model2 also includes a loop on Onboard and an xor between Payroll and skip

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The onboarding loop allows repeated onboarding of the same employee and the xor payroll construct allows payroll to be skipped entirely before closing the case

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: Skipping payroll is especially severe because a hired employee should be entered into payroll and omitting that undermines proper employment handling

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Model2 does at least enforce Interview before Decide but it fails to enforce Screen before Interview and introduces optional payroll and repeated onboarding

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Comparing both models Model1 has mainly one central structural error missing the dependency from Interview to Decide whereas Model2 has multiple deviations disconnected screening optional payroll and repeatable onboarding

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Therefore Model1 is closer to a normative Hire to Retire process because it preserves more of the expected mandatory sequence and does not permit skipping payroll or repeating onboarding
